55.query: Can praying mantises regenerate lost limbs and change body colour?

results.title: Evolutionary genomics of camouflage innovation in the orchid mantis
results.author: Guang-Ming Huang

results.title: Evolutionary basis and ecological function of body colour transition in the orchid mantis

results.title: Differentiation in the body color of Creobroter gemmata under red and green background at an early stage

results.author: L. Y. Rosenheim

results.title: The pigment transporter Redboy confers programmed body colour transition in orchid mantises
results.author: Xiaojin Pei

results.title: Morphology and material composition of raptorial foreleg cuticles in praying mantises Gongylus gongylodes and Sphodromantis lineola
results.author: Timo Zeimet
results.takeaway: Praying mantises' raptorial forelegs may adapt to their preferred prey type, with Sphodromantis lineola preferring harder prey and Gongylus gongylodes feeding on softer prey like flies.

157.query: Is animal pigmentation produced by enzymes such as tyrosinase acting on DOPA?

results.title: MAMMALIAN TYROSINASE AND DOPA OXIDASE
results.author: G. H. Hogeboom

results.author: M. Jiménez
results.takeaway: The brown and albino loci of mice encode similar tyrosinase proteins, suggesting that melanogenesis in mammals is controlled by multiple gene products.

results.title: Mammalian tyrosinase catalyzes three reactions in the biosynthesis of melanin.
results.author: A. Körner
results.takeaway: Mammalian tyrosinase plays a crucial role in the biosynthesis of melanin, with its unique ability to catalyze three distinct reactions within a single biochemical pathway.

results.title: Tyrosinase and related proteins in mammalian pigmentation
results.author: V. del Marmol
results.takeaway: Tyrosinase and related proteins play a crucial role in mammalian pigmentation by converting tyrosine to melanin, with recent findings suggesting a common ancestor for these genes.

results.title: Mammalian tyrosinase; preparation and properties.
results.author: A. Lerner
results.takeaway: Mammalian tyrosinase, an enzyme that catalyzes melanin production, has been confirmed to exist and is involved in the conversion of tyrosine to melanin in mammals.

61.query: Is there a universal quantitative law of arthropod growth across moult stages?

results.title: The Progression Factor in Insect Growth
results.author: F. S. Bodenheimer
results.takeaway: The growth factor in insect growth is a general rule of 2 for weight and i.26 for length, with deviations in complex cases being only deviations from this principle.

results.title: Dyar's rule and multivariate allometric growth in nine species of waterstriders (Heteroptera: Gerridae)
results.author: C. Klingenberg
results.takeaway: Dyar's rule is inconsistent in waterstrider species, but growth ratios vary within a limited range, with head width growth ratios differing more between moults than multivariate size.

results.title: The moulting arthropod: a complete genetic toolkit review
results.author: Giulia Campli
results.takeaway: Arthropods undergo periodic moulting events, requiring tightly regulated processes and highlighting knowledge gaps across diverse taxa.

results.title: DEVELOPMENTAL TRAIT EVOLUTION IN TRILOBITES
results.author: G. Fusco
results.takeaway: Trilobite developmental traits were relatively labile during evolutionary history, with growth rates varying independently among species and conforming to Dyar's rule.

results.title: Numerical Reduction of Instars in the Metamorphosis of Euphausids
results.author: R. Macdonald
results.takeaway: Euphausids undergo metamorphosis in stages, with each moult or ecdysis followed by rapid growth until the new chitinous layer hardens, resulting in a series of distinct life stages.

results.title: Model of copepod growth and development: moulting and mortality in relation to physiological processes during an individual moult cycle
results.author: F. Carlotti
results.takeaway: The mathematical model of copepod population dynamics demonstrates the relationship between physiological processes and population dynamics, allowing for predictions of population age structure and growth rates.

66.query: Can a regenerated arthropod antenna develop into a leg-like structure (homeosis)?

results.author: W. Gehring
results.journal: Journal of embryology and experimental morphology

results.title: A homoeotic mutation transforming leg to antenna in Drosophila
results.author: G. Struhl
results.takeaway: Homoeotic mutations in the Antp locus can transform the second leg into an antenna in Drosophila, promoting an atavistic condition.

results.title: Comparative analysis of leg and antenna development in wild-type and homeotic Drosophila melanogaster
results.author: M. Cummins
results.takeaway: The developmental programs of leg and antenna in Drosophila are partially conserved, but regulatory relationships and interactions between genes differ, resulting in positional specificity of homeotic transformations.

results.author: John H. Postlethwait
results.takeaway: The antennal-leg homoeotic mutant Antennapedia shows that cell division in antennae is influenced by both clonally inherited and environmental factors, suggesting two types of determination in antennal development.

results.title: Control of antennal versus leg development in Drosophila
results.author: F. Casares
results.takeaway: In Drosophila, Antennapedia promotes leg development by repressing homothorax and nuclear Exd, resulting in distinct antennal and leg structures.

results.title: Homeotic transformation of tail to limbs: A novel morphogenesis in the framework of self-organization and reprogramming of cell fate during appendage regeneration.
results.author: J. Hota

4.query: Does a single growth law describe body growth across different praying mantis species?

results.title: Allometric growth in the Praying mantis Stagmatoptera biocellata
results.author: Ernesto Rodriguez
results.takeaway: Allometric growth patterns in the Praying mantis Stagmatoptera biocellata show adaptative value and support the hypothesis of anatomic relationships between parts involved in the mantis strike.

results.title: Different paths to sexual size dimorphism in two praying mantids, Pseudomantis albofimbriata and Hierodula majuscula
results.author: Louise E. Allen

results.author: I. Hatton
results.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
results.takeaway: Metabolism and abundance scale with body size in a reciprocal manner across all eukaryotes, supporting energetic equivalence, while growth and mortality rates scale similarly across groups, contradicting a metabolic basis for growth and mortality scaling.

results.title: Manual of praying mantis morphology, nomenclature, and practices (Insecta, Mantodea)
results.author: Sydney K. Brannoch

results.title: Allometry of Ingestion Among Habitat Mimicking Praying Mantises
results.author: C. Oufiero
results.takeaway: Large praying mantises can ingest more resources than needed based on their metabolic rate, potentially impacting their role in the ecosystem.

results.title: The chromosome-level genome of Chinese praying mantis Tenodera sinensis (Mantodea: Mantidae) reveals its biology as a predator
results.author: Ruizhong Yuan
results.takeaway: The chromosome-level genome of the Chinese praying mantis reveals its biology as a predator, with expansions in digestion and detoxification genes, and a key role for LWS opsins in regulating predatory behaviors.

139.query: Does maternal age affect offspring body size in insects?

results.title: The influence of maternal age and mating frequency on egg size and offspring performance in Callosobruchus maculatus (Coleoptera: Bruchidae)
results.author: C. Fox

results.title: Triparental ageing in a laboratory population of an insect with maternal care

results.title: Variation in parasitoid adult size is related to host egg size, maternal state and developmental time
results.author: Mei Qi
results.takeaway: Anastatus disparis' body size is influenced by host egg size, maternal size, and developmental time, with larger females and shorter offspring development times.

results.title: Maternal rearing condition and age affect progeny fitness in the parasitoid wasp Lysiphlebus fabarum
results.author: P. Najafpour
results.takeaway: Progeny of Lysiphlebus fabarum parasitoid wasps with larger egg size and younger mothers have longer developmental time, with maternal body size being a stronger factor than age.

results.author: Stanislav Ožana

107.query: Does lifelong moulting preserve regenerative capacity in insects?

results.author: J. Michaud

results.title: Intestinal regeneration as an insect resistance mechanism to entomopathogenic bacteria.

results.title: The evolution of exoskeleton moulting in Trilobita
results.author: Harriet Drage

results.title: Physiology and Biochemistry of Insect Moulting Fluid

124.query: Can caterpillars regenerate larval appendages such as the caudal horn?

results.title: Physiological and molecular mechanisms of insect appendage regeneration
results.author: Jiru Zhong
results.takeaway: Insect appendage regeneration is influenced by physiological conditions and internal molecular mechanisms, with research progress focusing on the role of hormones and cytokines in this process.

results.title: Long non-coding RNAs involved in Drosophila development and regeneration

results.title: Chromatin status is key for regeneration.

results.title: Genetics and evolution of colour patterns in reptiles.

results.author: L. Legoff

results.title: Developmental origins of epigenetic transgenerational inheritance

results.title: Transgenerational inheritance: how impacts to the epigenetic and genetic information of parents affect offspring health.

results.title: Transgenerational epigenetic inheritance: a critical perspective

results.title: Adaptive colouration in amphibians.

results.author: Zhizhi Jing

results.author: R. P. Kostyuchenko

results.title: Evolutionary Aspects of Annelid Regeneration
results.author: V. G. Martinez-Acosta
results.takeaway: Annelids vary in their ability to regenerate body segments, with tail regeneration being nearly universal and head regeneration depending on the number of segments lost.

results.title: What Role Do Annelid Neoblasts Play? A Comparison of the Regeneration Patterns in a Neoblast-Bearing and a Neoblast-Lacking Enchytraeid Oligochaete
results.author: M. Myohara
results.takeaway: Annelid neoblasts are more essential for efficient asexual reproduction than for regeneration of missing body parts, as they do not show antero-posterior gradient of regeneration ability along the body axis.

results.title: Regeneration in Annelids
results.author: M. Zoran
results.takeaway: Annelids can regenerate tail segments nearly universally, but the ability to regenerate head segments varies among species, suggesting regeneration ability may be an ancient trait lost during evolution.

results.title: Bipolar head regeneration induced by artificial in (Annelida,
results.author: Shishin Kawamoto
results.takeaway: Bipolar head regeneration in the Enchytraeida Oligochaeta Enchytraeus japonensis worm occurs only after artificial amputation, with anesthesia before amputation increasing the frequency of bipolar head regeneration.

104.query: Does the environment induce body asymmetry in hermit crabs?

results.title: Environmentally induced subsymmetry in the hermit crab, Calcinus dapsiles Morgan (Decapoda, Diogenidae).
results.author: G. J. Morgan
results.takeaway: The hermit crab Calcinus dapsiles Morgan exhibits more symmetrical body shape and uropods than usual when living in a non-coiled shelter, suggesting environmental factors may influence their symmetry.

results.title: CENTRAL AND PERIPHERAL ORIGINS OF ASYMMETRY IN THE ABDOMINAL MOTOR SYSTEM OF THE HERMIT CRAB *
results.author: W. Chapple
results.journal: Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences
results.takeaway: Hermit crabs' abdominal nervous system shows asymmetries in different directions, highlighting the complexity of laterality and the adaptation of their body to their environment.

results.title: Asymmetrical morphology and growth of the hermit crab Pagurus filholi (Decapoda, Anomura, Paguridae) reared in non-dextral shell conditions
results.author: M. Imafuku
results.takeaway: Hermit crab Pagurus filholi shows asymmetrical morphology when reared in non-dextral shell conditions, with the right uropod becoming extended and the right major cheliped becoming enlarged.

results.title: Bergmann-Rensch Continuum Under Shell: Gender-Specific Trend in Response to Latitudinal Gradient
results.author: V. Vale
results.takeaway: Female hermit crabs show a gender-specific trend in body size in response to latitudinal gradients, suggesting that latitude increases the interpopulational body effect only in females.

results.title: Genes for asymmetry easily overruled
results.author: A. Harvey
results.takeaway: Hermit crab Clibanarius vittatus's asymmetry can be easily overridden by environmental factors, potentially aiding in the evolution of symmetrical king crabs and coconut crabs from hermit crab ancestors.

results.title: Mechanisms causing size differences of the land hermit crab Coenobita rugosus among eco-islands in Southern Taiwan
results.author: Chia-Hsuan Hsu
results.takeaway: Dongsha Island's land hermit crabs have a larger body size and faster growth due to their preference for seagrass over dicot leaves, suggesting a food hypothesis.

45.query: Can crustaceans regenerate specialized mouthpart appendages?

results.title: Autotomy and Regeneration of Appendages in Crustaceans: A Review
results.author: Lei Liu
results.journal: Journal of Ocean University of China
results.takeaway: Autotomy in crustaceans is a self-protection mechanism that can affect foraging efficiency, growth, and reproduction, with chelicerae removal reducing cannibalism in commercial production.

results.title: Functional Study of the BMP Signaling Pathway in Appendage Regeneration of Exopalaemon carinicauda
results.author: Chaofan Xing
results.takeaway: The BMP signaling pathway plays a critical role in the early stages of appendage regeneration in Exopalaemon carinicauda, affecting wound healing and blastema regeneration time.

results.title: Studies on the regeneration of crustacean appendages
results.author: G. Hooper
results.takeaway: Crustacean appendage regeneration shows a similar direction of differentiation to embryonic development, suggesting that social pressure influences the direction of differentiation.

results.title: Regenerated claws of the virile crayfish Faxonius virilis (Hagen, 1870) (Decapoda: Astacidea: Cambaridae) generate weaker pinching forces compared to original claws
results.author: Z. Graham
results.takeaway: Regenerated claws in virile crayfish Faxonius virilis generate weaker pinching forces (36% and 40% weaker in males and females) and have less muscle mass compared to original claws, potentially causing dishonest claws in crayfish.

results.title: CRISPR/Cas9 Mutagenesis Reveals Versatile Roles of Hox Genes in Crustacean Limb Specification and Evolution.
results.author: Arnaud Martin
results.takeaway: Hox genes play diverse roles in crustacean limb specification and evolution, with abdominal-A and Abdominal-B essential for proper posterior patterning and Ubx affecting gill development.

results.title: “Omics” data unveil early molecular response underlying limb regeneration in the Chinese mitten crab, Eriocheir sinensis
results.author: Jun Wang
results.takeaway: The Chinese mitten crab's strong limb regeneration is facilitated by signal transduction and immune response, with novel genetic pathways identified.

22.query: Does the amount of melanin change during colour change in fish?

results.title: A new insight into the pigmentation of the three-spined stickleback exposed to oxidative stress: day and night study
results.author: E. Sokołowska
results.takeaway: Fish skin pigmentation can be used to assess their welfare and detect environmental toxic agents, but only in dorsal skin during the day.

results.title: Pigmentation and colour changing mechanism in fishes
results.author: Mintumoni Pegu
results.takeaway: Fish pigmentation is influenced by genetic, morphological, physiological, and environmental factors, with genes and nutritional contents playing crucial roles in pigment production and color change.

results.title: Effects of background adaptation on the skin color of Malaysian red tilapia
results.author: Lan-mei Wang
results.takeaway: Malaysian red tilapia's skin color is more affected by background color than body color, with white background resulting in paler skin and black background resulting in darker skin.

results.title: Response of body color change rearing under different light intensity conditions in farmed red spotted grouper, Epinephelus akaara
results.author: Song-Hee Choi
results.takeaway: POMC mRNA and MC1R mRNA in red spotted grouper are involved in melanin synthesis and are controlled by light intensity, affecting body color changes during development.

results.title: Expression of genes for melanotropic peptides and their receptors for morphological color change in goldfish Carassius auratus.
results.author: K. Mizusawa
results.takeaway: Goldfish's morphological color changes are influenced by melanotropic peptides and their receptors, with mc5r playing a major role in reinforcing the function of MSH in morphological color changes.

results.title: Background matching through fast and reversible melanin-based pigmentation plasticity in tadpoles comes with morphological and antioxidant changes
results.author: H. C. Liedtke
results.takeaway: Tadpoles can rapidly and reversibly adjust their pigmentation to match environmental changes, leading to larger tail fins and improved redox status.

102.query: Do muscle proteins adapt their thermal stability to an animal's body temperature?

results.title: Thermal Stability of Contractile Proteins in Bat Wing Muscles Explains Differences in Temperature Dependence of Whole-Muscle Shortening Velocity
results.author: A. Rummel
results.takeaway: Bat wing muscles' contractile properties are influenced by the thermal stability of myofibrillar ATPase, with forearm muscles having less thermal stability than pectoralis muscles, but no significant difference in acute temperature dependence of enzyme activity.

results.title: Influence of seasonal variations on the physicochemical properties and thermal stability of myofibrillar proteins in snakehead (Channa argus).
results.author: Han Chen
results.takeaway: Snakehead muscle proteins show seasonal variations in composition, physicochemical properties, and thermal stability, with summer snakehead being more thermally stable and exhibiting better protein quality.

results.title: Physical and molecular bases of protein thermal stability and cold adaptation.
results.author: F. Pucci
results.takeaway: Protein thermal and cold stability and adaptation are complex and challenging, requiring further investigation for large-scale rational protein design.

results.title: Coping with Thermal Challenges: Physiological Adaptations to Environmental Temperatures
results.author: Glenn J. Tattersall
results.takeaway: Animals adapt to environmental temperatures through biochemical modifications in proteins, allowing for physiological adjustments to maintain optimal body temperatures or defend against constant temperature changes.

results.title: Dissecting the key residues crucial for the species-specific thermostability of muscle-type creatine kinase.
results.author: Y. Gao
results.takeaway: Two residues near the dimer interface of muscle-type creatine kinase, the 46th and 146th residue, are crucial for species-specific thermal stability.

results.title: Heat stability of fish muscle proteins
results.author: R. Poulter
results.journal: International Journal of Food Science and Technology
results.takeaway: Fish muscle proteins show varying heat stability, with more stable proteins found in fish found in higher ambient temperatures, while actins show similar thermal stability across all species.

43.query: Can birds regenerate beak or bill tissue?

results.title: Neurobiological basis of sensory perception: welfare implications of beak trimming.
results.author: W. Kuenzel
results.takeaway: Beak trimming in the poultry industry can prevent head, feather, and vent pecking, but should be conservative and limited to young birds to avoid neuromas and deformed beaks.

results.title: Anatomical consequences of partial beak amputation (beak trimming) in turkeys.
results.author: M. J. Gentle
results.takeaway: Beak trimming in turkeys leads to significant tissue loss, but healing and regrowth are similar across all methods, with the Bio-Beaker producing the most tissue damage.

results.title: Understanding How Infrared Beak Treatment Affects the Beak Tissue and the Healing Response of Brown and White Feathered Layer Pullets
results.author: S. Struthers
results.journal: Animals : an Open Access Journal from MDPI
results.takeaway: Infrared beak treatment effectively reduces beak growth and promotes healing in brown and white feathered layer chicks, with healing occurring by 21 days for both strains.

results.title: Cellular, Molecular, and Genetic Mechanisms of Avian Beak Development and Evolution
results.author: R. Schneider
results.takeaway: Avian beak development and evolution are influenced by cellular, molecular, and genetic mechanisms, with neural crest mesenchyme playing a key role in species-specific patterning and function.

results.title: Wound-Induced Regeneration in Feather Follicles: A Stepwise Strategy to Regenerate Stem Cells
results.author: T. Jiang
results.takeaway: Feather follicles show remarkable resilience in wound healing, regenerating new feathers after various injuries using a stepwise strategy involving stem cells in the collar bulge, papillary ectoderm, and de-differentiated lower follicle sheath.

results.title: Repair and Regeneration.
results.author: B. Purnell
results.takeaway: This special issue highlights research on repair and regeneration mechanisms, with an eye towards therapeutic applications, to enhance the body's ability to repair and regenerate damaged tissues and organs.
